name: Dr. Raghavender Medishetty
title: Assistant Professor, Department of Chemistry & Head of Department (Materials Science and Metallurgical Engineering)
bio: Dr. Raghavender Medishetty leads the SOMO Laboratory at IIT Bhilai, focusing on inorganic and metal-organic materials design. His research addresses global challenges through materials science and photochemistry, with emphasis on solid-state materials and devices for light-driven micromachines, photo-physical and nonlinear optics. He is recognized globally as a top 2% cited scientist and has published extensively in prestigious journals including Angewandte Chemie, Journal of Physical Chemistry Letters, and Chemical Communications.
education: PhD from National University of Singapore
Research Experience at Technical University of Munich, Germany
research: Development of crystalline materials (inorganic, organic, and metal-organic compounds) for photochemistry, semiconducting materials, and energy solutions. Two main research thrusts: (1) Solid-state photochemistry and molecular actuators using cycloaddition reactions to convert light into mechanical movement, and (2) Battery materials and degradation mechanisms for metal-ion battery systems.
achievements: Publications in Angewandte Chemie, JACS, Chemical Reviews, Advanced Materials
SERB and IIT Bhilai institutional funding recipient

portfolio.title: Light-Driven Molecular Actuators
portfolio.description: Development of photosalient crystals that convert light energy into mechanical motion through solid-state photochemistry.

portfolio.title: Metal-Organic Framework Photonics
portfolio.description: Multi-photon absorption, nonlinear optics, upconversion and lasing in metal-organic frameworks.

portfolio.title: Battery Materials Research
portfolio.description: Understanding degradation mechanisms in metal-ion batteries to extend operational lifetime for electric vehicles and energy storage.
